response:
We need to extract data and output JSON matching schema.

Fields:

- event_id: string: USGS Event ID: us7000jk2q

- magnitude: number: 6.4

- magnitude_type: string: "Mw" (from "6.4 Mw")

- time_utc: string: "2024-03-15 08:23:15 UTC"

- location: object with lat, lon, description.

Location text: "37.42N, 141.03E (off coast of Fukushima, Japan)". So lat = 37.42, lon = 141.03. description: "off coast of Fukushima, Japan". Could also include parentheses? We'll set description: "off coast of Fukushima, Japan".

- depth_km: number: 32

- tsunami: object with advisory: boolean, status: string.

Text: "Tsunami: Advisory issued, later cancelled". So advisory true? It says advisory issued, later cancelled. So advisory true. status maybe "cancelled". Could be "cancelled". We'll set advisory: true, status: "cancelled".

- intensity: array of objects each with location, mmi, description.

Text: "Intensity: MMI VI (Strong) in Iwaki, V (Moderate) in Sendai". So two entries:

1: location: "Iwaki", mmi: "VI", description: "Strong"

2: location: "Sendai", mmi: "V", description: "Moderate"

- felt_reports: number: "2,847 within 300km". So number 2847.

- nearby_cities: array of objects with name, distance_km.

Text: "Nearby cities: Iwaki (68km), Sendai (142km), Tokyo (287km)". So:

- Iwaki: 68 km

- Sendai: 142 km

- Tokyo: 287 km

Now produce JSON.

Check schema: event_id string, magnitude number, magnitude_type string, time_utc string, location object with lat number, lon number, description string, depth_km number, tsunami object with advisory boolean, status string, intensity array of objects with location string, mmi string, description string, felt_reports number, nearby_cities array of objects with name string, distance_km number.

All good.

Make sure JSON is valid, no trailing commas.

Let's craft:

  "magnitude": 6.4,
  "magnitude_type": "Mw",
  "time_utc": "2024-03-15 08:23:15 UTC",

    "lat": 37.42,

  },

  },

    },

    }
  ],
  "felt_reports": 2847,

    {
      "name": "Iwaki",
      "distance_km": 68
